issues.title: consider non-LocalNode alternatives
issues.author: ek345
issues.body:
By analogy, letting 127.0.0.0/8 or ::1 src/dst traffic onto the wire seems like not a great idea operationally.

I think it would be worth having the discussion about getting some alternate special-use names, like:
* `dtn:neighbor`
* `dtn:peer`
or something.

If we want to use DNS-compatible special names we could ask for `dtn:neighbor.dtn.arpa` or similar.  This would be simpler to delegate than the proposed `ipn.arpa` as there are no DNS infrastructure hosting requirements to consider -- it's just a reserved name under `.arpa`.

comments.author: ricktaylor
comments.body:
Yeah.. I considered `dtn:local` but then that brings in all of the dtn uri scheme to some extent.

The only relaxation we're making is on INGRESS.  localnode is still not allowed to leave the BPA...

issues.title: Consider GTSM for BP
issues.author: ek345
issues.body:
Similar to [RFC 5082](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5082) Generalized TTL Security Mechanism (GTSM), it might be a good security practice to require that both TTL fields in these BP-ARP messages are set to `1`, and declare all other values to be a protocol error requiring the Bundle to be discarded (and optionally logged).

IPv6 link-local unicast has a similar mechanism (checking for -1/255), but IPv6 link-local multicast performs this check:
* [MLDv1](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2710#section-3)
* [MLDv2](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3810#section-5)

comments.author: ricktaylor
comments.body:
I thought I had this in place... let me double check.

But, YES!

issues.title: received EIDs should be passed to the CLA for additional validation
issues.author: ek345
issues.body: When a BPA receives a BP-ARP reply, it `MUST` validate whether those EIDs are in fact within the scope of the TLS cert that was presented when the CL session was established.  Failure to do would be a pretty obvious attack vector, I would think.

comments.author: ricktaylor
comments.body: Hmm...  is that really needed.  The TLS cert is asserting the security and identity of the peer.  Does it matter if there are aliases published, over the established TLS channel + use BPSec?

issues.title: Eid return list integrity check and behaviour
issues.author: duratm
issues.body: In a case where what is returned is wrong (either because it is not well formed or incomplete data or other) what should be the behaviour of the bpa receiving this wrong response, should there be a retry, should this node be discarded because it is not responding as we expect it to ? Or this is not a specification that is needed and will be decided at the implementation level.

issues.title: Received Eid list in a Request check and behaviour
issues.author: duratm
issues.body: In the case where the sender of the Arp Request added an Eid list (to get an updated list of eid from the node). What the receiver should do if the list  is not right, for example if there are eids in this list that does not belong to this node, what should be the behavior of the receiving node (Given that only the delta is sent in the response to the requesting node).
